member.name: Sir John Hayes
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Sir John Hayes MP
member.role: MP for South Holland and The Deepings
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Sir John Hayes is the Conservative MP for South Holland and The Deepings , and has been an MP continually since 1 May 1997.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Tests
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what the average wait for a practical driving test has been at (a) Boston, (b) Grantham and (c) Peterborough test centres in each of the past three years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA) has now published more detailed data-sets so learners can get better understanding of the booking system. More information and waiting time data, including for Boston, Grantham and Peterborough driving test c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Tests: Lincolnshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many practical driving tests have been completed in Lincolnshire in each of the past three years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA) has now published more detailed data-sets so learners can get better understanding of the booking system. More information and waiting time data, including for Boston, Grantham and Peterborough driving test c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Business and Trade: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department uses numerical reasoning tests as part of the recruitment process for its graduate recruitment scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Business and Trade does not apply numerical reasoning tests as part of recruitment processes relating to graduate recruitment schemes.

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services: Care Homes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to help mprove access to dental services for for care home residents in Lincolnshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We recognise that certain groups of patients may be particularly vulnerable to oral health problems and may find it difficult to access dental care. Specialised dental services are in place to provide dental treatment in several settings, including care h...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Diabetes: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the UK's position globally in respect of research into beta cell preservation, regeneration and replacement therapies for type 1 diabetes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through UK Research and Innovation (UKRI), including UKRI’s Medical Research Council (MRC), the Government supports a broad portfolio of research relevant to Type 1 diabetes. This includes work on pancreatic biology, immunology, and cell replacement appro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her department uses numerical reasoning tests as part of the recruitment process for its graduate recruitment scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DSIT doesn't operate a departmental graduate recruitment scheme.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Treasury: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether her department uses numerical reasoning tests as part the recruitment process for its graduate recruitment scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Treasury’s graduate entry programme was paused for the 2025–26 cycle so that the department could undertake a procurement exercise for a new provider to run a replacement programme for September 2027 entry. The numerical reasoning test was removed from...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Education: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her department uses numerical reasoning tests as part of the recruitment process for its graduate recruitment scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department does not have a graduate recruitment scheme and instead places bids to provide placements for the Civil Service Fast Stream graduate recruitment scheme, which is managed centrally by the Cabinet Office.

writtenQuestions.heading: Defibrillators: South Holland and the Deepings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to increase defibrillator training in schools in South Holland and the Deepings constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defibrillators are designed to be simple and safe to use with no prior training. The devices talk users through step-by-step instructions at the time of use. ​As part of the department’s defibrillator programme, we provided awareness videos to schools sho...

writtenQuestions.heading: Wind Power: Birds
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her department has estimated how many seabirds have died due to colliding with offshore wind turbines for each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have commissioned the Joint Nature Conservation Committee to undertake a project to calculate estimated mortalities from offshore wind farms using up to date methods and parameters. This will support future assessments of offshore wind impacts on seabi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Ketamine: Drug Seizures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what quantity of ketamine has been seized at the UK border in each of the last ten years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to tackling drug harms through prevention, treatment and enforcement. We are working closely with police, health services and international partners to disrupt supply chains and protect communities.Data is available via drug se...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero: Public Appointments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many political appointees have been made by his Department since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have taken ‘political appointees’ to mean special advisers. A list of special advisers by department is published each year in the Annual Report on Special Advisers which is laid in parliament. The Annual Report on Special Advisers 2026 will be publish...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Release: Sexual Offences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many prisoners who will be made eligible for release halfway through their sentences were convicted of (1) rape and (2) sex offences.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I extend my sincere sympathy to all victims of these appalling crimes. This Government inherited a prisons system days from collapse. We had no choice but to take decisive action to stop our prisons overflowing and keep the public safe. Our landmark Sente...
